page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8ae.large is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The CPU is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and VAES. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.r8ae.large demonstrates strong single-core performance but exhibits limitations in multi-core scaling and database operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU test places the instance in the top 10% of its class with a score of 3111.99. However, its multi-core CPU performance is weak, scoring 3112.6, which falls into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for uncached large-block reads (45.05 GB/sec) and writes (26.33 GB/sec), while cached small-block reads are relatively weak at 153.11 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also on the lower end, with Redis achieving 2.8 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring in the bottom 10%.

This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require high memory capacity per core and strong single-threaded performance without the overhead of local storage or GPU accelerators. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the low multi-core scaling suggests a tradeoff where parallel processing is limited. The instance is best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications, lightweight caching layers, and general-purpose workloads that do not demand high multi-core throughput or intensive database transaction rates.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8ae.large is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 3.4 GHz AMD EPYC 9T34 processor on the x86_64 architecture. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 8.0 GB of memory per core and 32 MB of L3 cache, supporting advanced vector and cryptographic instructions like AVX-512 and AES. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core CPU performance, though multi-core scaling and database operations are weak. This profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy, single-threaded tasks. It is best suited for memory-intensive applications, lightweight caching, and workloads requiring high memory-to-vCPU ratios without intensive parallel processing demands.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high memory-to-core density for single-threaded workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio.
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications and lightweight caching.
